The facility video coverage was reviewed and staff was observed filling out the 15-minute supervision check paperwork and did not conduct at least 8 checks. || R501-19-3(8): Direct supervision only by direct care staff | Finding: The provider was out of compliance with this rule by failing to actively supervise a client with line-of-sight check-ins. The client had left the treatment facility at approximately 9:23pm and their absence was not discovered until the following morning at approximately 7am by a client(s).

Inspection 2 Findings:
R380-80-5(4): Provider shall protect clients from abuse, prevent abuse | Finding: The provider was out of compliance with R380-80-5(4) by not protecting a client from  any action that may compromise the health and safety of the client through acts or omissions. During the inspection, the investigator determined that the provider failed to summon emergency medical services for a minor client who was rendered unconscious and sustained severe head and facial injuries during a physical assault. Rather than summoning emergency medical transport, the provider elected to call a non-medical transport company to take the client to the hospital, delaying medical treatment || R380-80-6(1)(a)-(i): Client rights | Finding: The provider was out of compliance with R380-80-6(1)(c) for failing to ensure that each client has the right to be free from potential harm or acts of violence. During the inspection, the investigator determined that the provider failed to act upon explicit safety concerns, peer threats of violence, and unit transfer requests communicated by a minor client and his legal guardian prior to a severe physical attack. The therapist confirmed these concerns and requests were brought to his attention, but the client was not moved. 

 || R380-80-6(1)(a)-(i): Client rights | Finding: The provider was out of compliance with R380-80-6(1)(c) for failing to ensure that each client has the right to be free from potential harm or acts of violence. During the inspection, the investigator viewed the video footage which showed a client pour water on another client’s head and slap the top of his head. A staff member attempted to intervene, extending her arm, but was pushed aside. Staff failed to use behavior management training and perform appropriate holds and restraints in order to prevent harm to the client. That staff member called on the radio for assistance while the client lifted the other client up, slammed him onto the floor, and punched him in the face four times rendering him unconscious sustaining severe facial injuries and life threatening head injuries.

R501-1-10(5): No withholding or manipulating sources | Finding: The provider was out of compliance with this rule by withholding and manipulating information During on and off-site inspections, licensors reviewed 2 unrelated incident reports, written by staff members, that omitted client physical mistreatment. This is a repeat noncompliance issue. || R495-876-5(1)(a)-(m): Code of conduct | Finding: The provider was out of compliance with this rule on two separate occasions by failing to comply with mandatory reporting laws by:
1. Failing to report that a child was physically mistreated and seriously injured by a staff member on 01/19/2022. Youth Health Associates did not report to the applicable investigative agency.

During off-site inspections, licensors observed 8 critical incident reports that were not reported accurately or at all. These incidents included a client medical emergency as a result of reportedly smoking methamphetamine, an unnecessary and aggressive physical restraint that resulted in serious client injury, a physical restraint in which a staff punched a client in the face multiple times that resulted in client injury, 5 incidents of client sexual misconduct, criminal activity, and an AWOL incident. This is a repeat non-compliance concern. || R501-14-5(2)(a)-(d): Submitting application for an initial background screening | Finding: The provider was out of compliance with this rule on 2 separate occasions:
1. by failing to submit an application no later than two weeks from the applicant becoming associated with the license. During an off-site inspection, licensors observed the DACS background screening system and noted that Youth Health Associates had not submitted an application for a staff member who worked at the facility.
2. by allowing 5 staff members without eligible background clearances to work without being directly supervised. During on and off-site inspections, licensors verified that the provider allowed 5 current staff, without eligible background clearances, to work without constant audio and visual supervision from a staff with an eligible background clearance. || R495-876-6(1)(a)-(g): Client rights | Finding: The provider was out of compliance with this rule by compromising the health and safety of a client. During an on-site inspection, licensors conducted multiple interviews. Evidence substantiated that on 10/30/2023, a client was made to sit on the floor of the van, without a seatbelt, while a staff member drove the provider’s van during an outing.
